findings.description: Page 2 of the HTML rendering inserts a large blank space where the PDF has continuous content. The PDF page 1 content flows directly, but the HTML splits it with a repeated header/footer and excessive whitespace before continuing on a new 'page'.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 ends with Onslow County section C (Queens Creek). HTML page 2 shows mostly blank space with repeated letterhead before continuing to page 2 content.

findings.description: The Roman numeral list indentation structure in the HTML is flattened compared to the PDF. In the PDF, items I-IX have sub-items indented under county headings with clear hierarchical indentation. The HTML uses a simpler indentation scheme.
findings.evidence: PDF shows deeply nested structure with Roman numerals at left margin, county names indented, and sub-items (A, B, C...) further indented. HTML shows a flatter hierarchy.

findings.description: The North Carolina Marine Fisheries logo/seal appears slightly different in rendering between PDF and HTML but is present in both.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the seal at top-left of page 1. HTML also shows it at top-left but with slightly different sizing.

findings.description: Fabricated link URL for NCDMF Interactive Map
findings.evidence: The PDF mentions 'visit the NCDMF Interactive Map' as a hyperlink but the HTML contains the specific URL 'https://ncdenr.maps.arcgis.com/home/webmap/viewer.html?useExisting=1&layers=671ea4b8ffc0456fa64b3d02d16e31fa' which cannot be verified from the PDF text alone. This URL may have been extracted from the underlying PDF link data, but if fabricated it would be critical.

findings.description: Fabricated link URL for PT-1-2024 proclamation
findings.evidence: The HTML contains 'https://files.nc.gov/deq/documents/2023-12/PT-1-2024%20Amend%20Crabpot%20Restrictions.pdf?VersionId=Heo3U_yvo9FJRZHcBP28ktcORB3PGqCy' for the PT-1-2024 link. The PDF text only says 'See proclamation PT-1-2024'. This specific URL with version ID cannot be verified from the PDF text.

The HTML contains 'https://deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/marine-fisheries/rules-proclamations-and-size-and-bag-limits/fisheries-management-proclamations#currentprocs' for the 'latest proclamation' link. While the PDF has this as a hyperlink, the specific URL cannot be confirmed from the PDF text alone.

findings.description: Only one heading level used - no hierarchy beyond h1
findings.evidence: The HTML uses only h1 for 'PROCLAMATION'. All section headings (Roman numeral sections like CARTERET COUNTY/ONSLOW COUNTY, ONSLOW COUNTY, PENDER COUNTY, etc.) are rendered as bold/underlined text within list items rather than as proper headings (h2, h3). While the PDF doesn't use explicit heading formatting either, the document structure with major Roman numeral sections would benefit from heading hierarchy.
The HTML contains a <main> element inside the outer <main> from the template wrapper, creating invalid nesting, though this is a structural HTML issue rather than content fidelity.
Both PDF and HTML contain 'IT SHALL BE UNLAWFULLTO USE POTS' - the typo is faithfully reproduced, which is correct behavior.

findings.description: Alt text for header image is reasonably descriptive but not fully verifiable
findings.evidence: The header image alt text says 'North Carolina Marine Fisheries and State Seal logos' - the PDF shows the NC Marine Fisheries seal/logo. The description appears reasonable.

findings.description: Generic alt text for footer images
findings.evidence: Footer images have alt='Page footer graphic' which is generic but acceptable since the actual footer content appears to be decorative.
